content:
# Joshua Chapter 15 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 238-240 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_015_page_001.jpg`

---

1 And the lot for the tribe of the sons of Yehudah according to their clans was to the border of Edom, the wilderness of Tzin southward, from the end of the Negev.

2 And their southern border was from the end of the Salt Sea, from the tongue that faces southward.

3 And it went out to the south of the ascent of Aqrabbim, and passed over to Tzin, and went up from the south to Qadesh-Barnea, and passed over [to] Chetzron, and went up to Addar, and turned around [to] the Qarqa.

4 And it passed over to Atzmon, and went out to the Wadi of Mitzrayim, and the goings out of the border were to the sea. This shall be your southern border.

5 And the eastern border [was] the Salt Sea unto the end of the Yarden. And the border on the northern side [was] from the tongue of the sea, from the end of the Yarden.

6 And the border went up [to] Bet-Choglah, and passed over from the north of Bet-ha-Aravah, and the border went up [to] the stone of Bohan son of Re'uven.

7 And the border went up to Devir from the valley of Akhor, and northward turning toward the Gilgal which [is] opposite the ascent of Adummim, which [is] from the south of the wadi, and the border passed over to the waters of Ein-Shemesh, and its goings out were to Ein-Rogel.

8 And the border went up [by] the valley of Ben-Hinnom to the side of the Yevusi from the south — it [is] Yerushalayim — and the border went up to the top of the mountain which [is] before the valley of Hinnom westward, which [is] at the end of the valley of Repha'im northward.

9 And the border marked out from the top of the mountain to the spring of the waters of Nephtoach, and went out to the cities of Mount Ephron, and the border marked out [to] Ba'alah — it [is] Qiryat-Ye'arim.

10 And the border turned around from Ba'alah westward to Mount Se'ir, and passed over to the side of Mount Ye'arim from the north — it [is] Kesalon — and went down [to] Bet-Shemesh, and passed over [to] Timnah.

11 And the border went out to the side of Eqron northward, and the border marked out [to] Shikkeron, and passed over [to] Mount Ba'alah, and went out [to] Yavne'el; and the goings out of the border were to the sea.

12 And the western border [was] the Great Sea and [its] coastline. This [is] the border of the sons of Yehudah all around, according to their clans.

13 And to Kalev son of Yephunneh he gave a portion in the midst of the sons of Yehudah, according to the mouth of YHWH to Yehoshua: Qiryat-Arba — the father of the Anaq — it [is] Chevron.

14 And Kalev drove out from there the three sons of the Anaq: Sheshai and Achiman and Talmai, the children of the Anaq.

15 And he went up from there to the inhabitants of Devir — and the name of Devir formerly [was] Qiryat-Sepher.

16 And Kalev said: "Whoever strikes Qiryat-Sepher and captures it, and I will give to him Akhsah my daughter as a wife."

17 And Otni'el son of Qenaz, brother of Kalev, captured it; and he gave to him Akhsah his daughter as a wife.

18 And it was, when she came, and she urged him to ask from her father a field. And she got down from upon the donkey, and Kalev said to her: "What [is it] to you?"

19 And she said: "Give to me a blessing, for the land of the Negev you have given me, and give to me springs of water." And he gave to her the upper springs and the lower springs.

20 This [is] the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Yehudah according to their clans.

21 And the cities at the end of the tribe of the sons of Yehudah toward the border of Edom in the Negev were: Qavtze'el and Eder and Yagur,

22 and Qinah and Dimonah and Ad'adah,

23 and Qedesh and Chatzor and Yitnan,

24 Ziph and Telem and Be'alot,

25 and Chatzor-Chadattah and Qeriyyot-Chetzron — it [is] Chatzor —

26 Amam and Shema and Moladah,

27 and Chatzar-Gaddah and Cheshmon and Bet-Pelet,

28 and Chatzar-Shu'al and Be'er-Sheva and Bizyotyah,

29 Ba'alah and Iyyim and Etzem,

30 and Eltolad and Kesil and Chormah,

31 and Tziqlag and Madmannah and Sansannah,

32 and Levaot and Shilchim and Ayin and Rimmon: all the cities [were] twenty and nine, and their villages.

33 In the Shephelah: Eshta'ol and Tzor'ah and Ashnah,

34 and Zanoach and Ein-Gannim, Tappuach and ha-Enam,

35 Yarmut and Adullam, Sokho and Azeqah,

36 and Sha'arayim and Aditayim and ha-Gederah and Gederotayim: fourteen cities and their villages.

37 Tzenan and Chadashah and Migdal-Gad,

38 and Dil'an and ha-Mitzpeh and Yoqte'el,

39 Lakhish and Botzqat and Eglon,

40 and Kabbon and Lachmas and Kitlish,

41 and Gederot, Bet-Dagon and Na'amah and Maqqedah: sixteen cities and their villages.

42 Livnah and Eter and Ashan,

43 and Yiphtach and Ashnah and Netziv,

44 and Qe'ilah and Akhziv and Mareshah: nine cities and their villages.

45 Eqron and her daughters and her villages,

46 from Eqron and westward, all that [were] beside Ashdod and their villages.

47 Ashdod, her daughters and her villages; Azzah, her daughters and her villages, unto the Wadi of Mitzrayim, and the Great Sea and [its] coastline.

48 And in the hill country: Shamir and Yattir and Sokho,

49 and Dannah and Qiryat-Sannah — it [is] Devir —

50 and Anav and Eshtemoh and Anim,

51 and Goshen and Cholon and Giloh: eleven cities and their villages.

52 Arav and Dumah and Esh'an,

53 and Yanum and Bet-Tappuach and Apheqah,

54 and Chumtah and Qiryat-Arba — it [is] Chevron — and Tzi'or: nine cities and their villages.

55 Ma'on, Karmel and Ziph and Yuttah,

56 and Yizre'el and Yoqde'am and Zanoach,

57 the Qayin, Giv'ah and Timnah: ten cities and their villages.

58 Chalchul, Bet-Tzur and Gedor,

59 and Ma'arat and Bet-Anot and Elteqon: six cities and their villages.

60 Qiryat-Ba'al — it [is] Qiryat-Ye'arim — and ha-Rabbah: two cities and their villages.

61 In the wilderness: Bet-ha-Aravah, Middin and Sekakhah,

62 and ha-Nivshan and Ir-ha-Melach and Ein-Gedi: six cities and their villages.

63 And the Yevusi, the inhabitants of Yerushalayim — the sons of Yehudah were not able to drive them out; and the Yevusi dwelt with the sons of Yehudah in Yerushalayim unto this day.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v1 — "Tzin" (צִן):** Transliterated directly; this is the wilderness region at the southern extremity of Canaan, distinct from "Sin" (סִין) near Egypt.

2. **v3 — "Aqrabbim" (עַקְרַבִּים):** Literally "scorpions." The "ascent of Aqrabbim" (מַעֲלֵה עַקְרַבִּים) = "ascent of scorpions," a pass south of the Dead Sea. The name is preserved untranslated as a proper place name.

3. **v3 — "Qarqa" (הַקַּרְקָעָה):** With the definite article in Hebrew. Some MSS read differently; LC has הקרקעה.

4. **v7 — "Gilgal" (הַגִּלְגָּל):** Not the famous Gilgal near Jericho but a different site near the ascent of Adummim. The definite article is present in Hebrew.

5. **v8 — "valley of Ben-Hinnom" (גֵּי בֶן־הִנֹּם):** The infamous valley south of Jerusalem, later known as Gehenna (גֵּיהִנֹּם). Translated literally as "valley of the son of Hinnom."

6. **v9 — "marked out" (תָּאַר):** The verb תאר means "to outline, mark out, delineate" a boundary — a distinct term from the standard "went up/went out" boundary language.

7. **v13 — "father of the Anaq" (אֲבִי הָעֲנָק):** The Hebrew אבי הענק can mean "father of Anaq" (i.e., Arba was the progenitor) or "the great one among the Anaq." Translated literally.

8. **v18 — "she urged him" (וַתְּסִיתֵהוּ):** The verb סות means "to incite, urge, entice." Akhsah urged Otni'el to request land from her father, then she herself dismounted to make the appeal.

9. **v25 — Qeriyyot-Chetzron:** Written as a compound name in the Hebrew (קְרִיּוֹת חֶצְרוֹן); the parenthetical gloss "it [is] Chatzor" identifies this as another name for Chatzor.

10. **v32 — City count:** The text states "twenty and nine" (עֶשְׂרִים וָתֵשַׁע), but counting the names in vv. 21-32 yields more than twenty-nine. This is a well-known textual difficulty; some names may be sub-localities or compound entries (e.g., Chatzor-Chadattah, Qeriyyot-Chetzron = Chatzor).

11. **v33 — "Shephelah" (הַשְּׁפֵלָה):** The lowland foothill region between the coastal plain and the Judean hill country. Retained as a transliteration since it is a recognized geographic term.

12. **v45-47 — "daughters" (בְּנוֹתֶיהָ):** Literally "her daughters," meaning dependent towns or satellite settlements surrounding a major city. This is standard Hebrew geographic terminology.

13. **v57 — "the Qayin" (הַקַּיִן):** With the definite article, indicating a place name, not the personal name Cain. Some translations read "Kain."

14. **v63 — "unto this day" (עַד הַיּוֹם הַזֶּה):** This editorial note places the composition (or final editing) of this text before David's conquest of Jerusalem (2 Samuel 5:6-9), when the Jebusites still occupied the city alongside Judahites.

15. **Conjunctions preserved:** The Hebrew waw-consecutive (וַ/וְ) introducing nearly every verse and city name is rendered consistently as "and," maintaining the characteristic Hebrew narrative chain structure throughout the boundary descriptions and city lists.
